mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so monthly declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after monthly FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, so an increase in unemployment above recent trend predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both weaken, housing and retail demand are simultaneously slowing, which should pressure cyclical earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO reflects weaker industrial output and profit-sensitive real activity, so monthly contractions in production should be followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 5-day SPX returns are not negative on average, or if the signal loses directional accuracy below 50% in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation surprises that push CPIAUCSL higher while FEDFUNDS remains very low imply policy stays accommodative relative to prices, supporting risk assets and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL is rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate and rising-CPI condition does not yield above-baseline positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in any 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth signals expanding payroll income and consumer demand, so sustained monthly payroll gains predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month payroll growth is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if predictive accuracy falls below 55% in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad final-demand weakness, which should compress earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weak GDP and retail demand do not forecast negative 60-day returns better than either series alone, or if the signal's sign is not stable across rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO combined with low unemployment reflects improving operating leverage and earnings momentum, which should lead to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if the joint production-up and unemployment-down condition does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the interaction effect becomes negative in any 2-year rolling window.
